imp: verb. To engraft or plant (a plant or part of one, a sapling, etc.).
imply: verb. To have as a necessary consequence; to lead to (something) as a consequence.
lilt: verb. To do something rhythmically, with animation and quickness, usually of music.
lily: noun. Any of several flowers in the genus Lilium of the family Liliaceae, which includes a great many ornamental species.
limp: verb. To walk lamely, as if favoring one leg.
limply: adverb. In a limp manner, without support or resistance.
lip: noun. Either of the two fleshy protrusions around the opening of the mouth.
lit: verb. simple past and past participle of light (“illuminate; start a fire; etc”)
lum: noun. A chimney; also, the top part of a chimney.
lump: noun. Something that protrudes, sticks out, or sticks together; a cluster or blob; a mound or mass of no particular shape.
lumpily: adverb. In a lumpy manner, with lumps.
lumpy: adjective. Full of lumps, not smooth, uneven.
mil: noun. An angular mil, a unit of angular measurement equal to ⁄ 6400 of a complete circle.
mill: noun. A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc. (Some are small and simple, and some are large and complex.)
milt: noun. The spleen, especially of an animal bred for food.
milty: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of milt.
mity: adjective. Alternative spelling of mitey (“infested with mites”).
mull: verb. To work (over) mentally; to cogitate; to ruminate.
multiply: verb. To increase the amount, degree or number of (something).
pill: noun. A small, usually round or cylindrical object designed for easy swallowing, usually containing some sort of medication.
pit: noun. A hole in the ground.
pity: noun. A feeling of sympathy at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.
piu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pintupi-Luritja.
plum: noun. The fruit and its tree.
plumy: adjective. Covered or adorned with plumes, or as with plumes; feathery.
pul: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of an Afghan afghani.
puli: noun. One of a breed of Hungarian sheepdog with a distinctive thick, corded coat.
pull: verb. To apply a force to (an object) so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force.
put: verb. To physically place (sth or sb swh).
till: preposition. Until; to, up to; as late as (a given time).
tip: noun. The extreme end of something, especially when pointed; e.g. the sharp end of a pencil.
tui: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tupuri.
tulip: noun. A type of flowering plant, genus Tulipa.
tup: noun. A male sheep, a ram.
uplit: verb. simple past and past participle of uplight
yum: interjection. Indicating delight at the flavor of food.
yup: noun. A yes; an affirmative answer.
imu: noun. A traditional Hawaiian underground oven, consisting mainly of a hole in the ground.
limpy: adjective. Having or resembling a limp.
limu: noun. Any of various kinds of edible algae, which form an important part of the traditional Hawaiian diet.
llm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lasalimu.
pil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yom.
plu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Palikur.
puy: noun. Any of several cone-shaped hills in the Auvergne, France that are the remains of extinct volcanos.
tilly: noun. An extra product given to a customer at no additional charge; a lagniappe.
tim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Timbe.
tipu: noun. A South American tree, Tipuana tipu, which is grown as an ornamental in mild-winter climates.
tulipy: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a tulip.
tymp: noun. A hollow water-cooled iron casting in the upper part of the archway of a blast furnace in which the dam stands.
ull: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ullatan.
util: noun. A hypothetical unit measuring satisfaction.
yilt: noun. Alternative form of gilt (“young female pig, at or nearing the age of first breeding”).
yump: verb. To leave the ground briefly when driving over a ridge at high speed.
